content.text:
Now I have comprehensive competitive intelligence and industry knowledge. Let me perform a deep audit of the Financial Management & Budget module based on the codebase snapshot and all context provided.

```json
{
  "module": "financial-engine",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 32,
    "domain_depth": 28,
    "data_richness": 22,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 12,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 40,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 38,
    "realtime": 25,
    "accessibility": 18,
    "security": 30,
    "differentiation": 15,
    "enterprise_readiness": 22
  },
  "overall_score": 25,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Pay Application (AIA G702/G703) workflow is not implemented as an interactive data-entry module",
      "description": "The codebase has a PaymentApplications page and PDF export components (G702ApplicationPDF.tsx, G703ContinuationPDF.tsx), but there is no interactive Schedule of Values (SOV) data entry table, no percent-complete editing per line item, no stored materials column, no retainage calculation engine, and no pay app state machine for the draft → submitted → reviewed → approved → paid lifecycle. Procore's entire financial module is anchored on this workflow. A PM cannot create, edit, or submit a pay application today. This is the single most critical financial workflow for every GC — it directly controls cash flow. Without a real AIA billing workflow, no CFO will trust this platform.",
      "prompt": "You are building the AIA Pay Application (G702/G703) interactive workflow for SiteSyncAI, the most advanced construction project management platform.

## TASK
Build a complete, interactive Pay Application module with Schedule of Values entry, percent-complete editing, retainage calculation, stored materials tracking, and a full lifecycle state machine.

## FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY

### 1. Create `src/machines/payAppMachine.ts`
Create an XState v5 state machine with states: `draft`, `submitted`, `under_review`, `approved`, `paid`, `rejected`, `void`. Transitions:
- draft → submitted (event: SUBMIT, requires all line items to have valid percent complete)
- submitted → under_review (event: START_REVIEW)
- under_review → approved (event: APPROVE, requires reviewer signature)
- under_review → rejected (event: REJECT, requires rejection reason)
- approved → paid (event: MARK_PAID, records payment date and check number)
- any → void (event: VOID, requires reason)
- rejected → draft (event: REVISE)
Context must include: payAppNumber, billingPeriodStart, billingPeriodEnd, contractId, lineItems[], retainageRate (default 0.10), totalScheduledValue, totalCompletedPrevious, totalCompletedThisPeriod, totalStoredMaterials, totalCompletedAndStored, totalRetainage, totalNetPayment, submittedBy, submittedAt, approvedBy, approvedAt.

### 2. Create `src/types/payApp.ts`
Define TypeScript interfaces:
```typescript
interface PayAppLineItem {
  id: string;
  lineNumber: number;
  description: string;
  scheduledValue: number; // from SOV
  previousApplications: number; // sum of all prior approved pay apps for this line
  workCompletedThisPeriod: number; // dollar amount completed this billing period
  materialsStoredThisPeriod: number; // stored materials not yet installed
  totalCompletedAndStored: number; // calculated: previousApplications + workCompletedThisPeriod + materialsStoredThisPeriod
  percentComplete: number; // calculated: totalCompletedAndStored / scheduledValue * 100
  balanceToFinish: number; // calculated: scheduledValue - totalCompletedAndStored
  retainage: number; // calculated: totalCompletedAndStored * retainageRate
  costCode: string; // CSI division code
  changeOrderIds: string[]; // linked COs that modified this line
}

interface PayApplication {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  payAppNumber: number;
  applicationDate: string;
  billingPeriodStart: string;
  billingPeriodEnd: string;
  contractValue: number;
  changeOrderTotal: number;
  revisedContractValue: number;
  lineItems: PayAppLineItem[];
  retainageRate: number;
  retainageReleaseRate: number; // for substantial completion release
  status: 'draft' | 'submitted' | 'under_review' | 'approved' | 'paid' | 'rejected' | 'void';
  previousPayApps: { number: number; amount: number; date: string }[];
  totalEarnedToDate: number;
  totalRetainageToDate: number;
  currentPaymentDue: number;
  submittedBy: string | null;
  submittedAt: string | null;
  approvedBy: string | null;
  approvedAt: string | null;
  notes: string;
}
```

### 3. Create `src/components/financial/PayAppEditor.tsx`
Build a full-width editable table (not a generic DataTable — this needs a spreadsheet-like experience):
- Column headers must match AIA G703 exactly: A (Item No.), B (Description of Work), C (Scheduled Value), D (Work Completed From Previous Application), E (Work Completed This Period), F (Materials Presently Stored), G (Total Completed and Stored to Date D+E+F), H (% Complete G÷C), I (Balance to Finish C-G), J (Retainage)
- Each cell in columns E and F must be inline-editable (click to edit, Tab to next cell, Enter to confirm)
- Column H (% Complete) must update in real-time as E and F change
- Column G, I, J must be auto-calculated and non-editable (gray background)
- Footer row shows column totals
- Validation: percentComplete cannot exceed 100%, workCompletedThisPeriod cannot be negative, totalCompletedAndStored cannot exceed scheduledValue
- Red highlight on any cell that fails validation
- Summary section above the table matching G702 format: Original Contract Sum, Net Change by Change Orders, Contract Sum to Date, Total Completed and Stored to Date, Retainage (a. X% of Completed Work, b. X% of Stored Material), Total Earned Less Retainage, Less Previous Certificates for Payment, Current Payment Due, Balance to Finish Including Retainage
- All summary values must be calculated from line item data — never hardcoded
- Use the design system: 48px row height, 14px Inter font, #E5E7EB borders, sticky header, sticky first two columns for horizontal scroll
- Primary CTA button: 'Submit Pay Application' (orange, disabled until all validations pass)

### 4. Create `src/components/financial/PayAppSummary.tsx`
A G702 summary card component showing:
- Application number and billing period
- 5 metric cards: Contract Sum to Date, Total Earned, Retainage Held, Previous Payments, Current Payment Due
- Status badge with colored dot per design system
- Timeline showing lifecycle events (created, submitted, reviewed, approved, paid) with dates and users

### 5. Modify `src/pages/PaymentApplications.tsx`
Replace the existing page with:
- Top metric cards row: Total Billed to Date, Outstanding Retainage, Current Period Billing, Collection Rate (days from submission to payment), Next Billing Deadline
- Tab navigation: All Pay Apps | Draft | Submitted | Approved | Paid
- Table of pay applications: Number, Period, Status, Amount Billed, Retainage, Net Payment, Submitted Date, Approved Date
- Each row clickable to PayAppEditor
- 'Create New Pay Application' button that auto-populates line items from the current SOV and change orders, carrying forward previous application amounts from the last approved pay app
- Empty state with construction-specific message and illustration

### 6. Update `src/api/endpoints/budget.ts`
Add functions: getPayApplications(), getPayApplicationById(id), createPayApplication(data), updatePayApplication(id, data), submitPayApplication(id). Each must query/mutate a `pay_applications` table and `pay_app_line_items` table.

### 7. Update `src/api/queryKeys.ts`
Add payApplications query keys.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A PM can create a new pay app that auto-populates SOV from budget line items + approved change orders
- Previous application amounts are automatically carried forward from the last approved pay app
- Inline editing of work completed and stored materials with real-time calculations
- All AIA G702 summary calculations are correct to the penny
- Retainage is calculated per line item (not just on the total)
- The pay app can be submitted, reviewed, approved, and marked as paid
- PDF export generates a document matching AIA G702/G703 format (leverage existing G702ApplicationPDF.tsx and G703ContinuationPDF.tsx)
- Every number on screen is a real calculated value, never a placeholder
- Tab key navigates between editable cells like a spreadsheet
- Works on tablet (iPad) for field use during walkthrough percent-complete verification"
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "13-week cash flow forecast is completely missing",
      "description": "The product vision calls for '13-week cash flow from committed cost data' and the competitive analysis identifies this as a key gap in Procore. The codebase has a CashFlowWidget.tsx but it appears to be a simple dashboard widget, not a full cash flow forecasting engine. There is no cash flow projection model, no inflow/outflow tracking, no weekly granularity, and no scenario modeling. Bauwise and Kahua both offer sophisticated cash flow forecasting. Siteline offers automatic payment date prediction. SiteSync has nothing close to this. A CFO cannot make a single cash decision with the current state.",
      "prompt": "You are building the 13-Week Cash Flow Forecasting Engine for SiteSyncAI, the most advanced construction project management platform.

## TASK
Build a complete 13-week cash flow forecasting system that projects weekly inflows and outflows from real project data (committed costs, approved pay apps, pending change orders, sub payment schedules, retainage).

## FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY

### 1. Create `src/lib/cashFlowEngine.ts`
Build a pure-function cash flow projection engine:
```typescript
interface CashFlowWeek {
  weekNumber: number;
  weekStarting: string; // ISO date
  weekEnding: string;
  // Inflows
  ownerPayments: number; // from approved pay apps, offset by collection days
  retainageReleases: number; // scheduled retainage releases
  otherInflows: number;
  totalInflows: number;
  // Outflows  
  subcontractorPayments: number; // from approved sub invoices, offset by payment terms
  materialPurchases: number; // from PO schedule
  laborCosts: number; // from crew data
  equipmentCosts: number;
  generalConditions: number; // insurance, temp facilities, etc.
  retainagePayments: number; // retainage released to subs
  otherOutflows: number;
  totalOutflows: number;
  // Net
  netCashFlow: number; // totalInflows - totalOutflows
  cumulativePosition: number; // running total from starting balance
  // Risk
  confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; // based on how certain the data is
}

interface CashFlowForecast {
  projectId: string;
  generatedAt: string;
  startingBalance: number;
  weeks: CashFlowWeek[];
  summary: {
    totalProjectedInflows: number;
    totalProjectedOutflows: number;
    netPosition: number;
    lowestCashPoint: { week: number; amount: number };
    peakCashNeed: { week: number; amount: number };
  };
  assumptions: string[]; // human-readable assumptions
}

function generateCashFlowForecast(params: {
  startingBalance: number;
  payApps: PayApplication[]; // approved but unpaid
  subContracts: SubContract[]; // with payment terms
  purchaseOrders: PurchaseOrder[];
  crewCosts: WeeklyLaborCost[];
  retainageSchedule: RetainageRelease[];
  collectionDays: number; // typically 30
  paymentTermsDays: number; // typically 30 after GC receives payment
  changeOrders: ChangeOrder[]; // pending COs with probability weighting
}): CashFlowForecast
```
The engine must:
- Calculate owner payment inflows by offsetting approved pay app dates by collectionDays
- Calculate sub payment outflows by offsetting sub invoice dates by paymentTermsDays
- Spread committed but not-yet-invoiced costs across remaining weeks based on schedule percent complete
- Weight pending change orders by probability (approved=100%, pending=50%, draft=10%)
- Mark confidence level per week: 'high' for weeks 1-4 (mostly known), 'medium' for weeks 5-8, 'low' for weeks 9-13
- Calculate the lowest cash point (the week where cumulative position is minimum — this is critical for the CFO)

### 2. Create `src/components/financial/CashFlowChart.tsx`
Build a Recharts area chart:
- X-axis: 13 weeks labeled 'Week 1 (Apr 7)' etc.
- Two filled areas: green for inflows, red for outflows
- A bold line showing cumulative net position
- A horizontal dashed line at $0 (the danger line)
- If cumulative position goes below $0 at any point, highlight that zone in red with a warning label 'Cash Shortfall: $X'
- Tooltip showing all line items for the hovered week
- Confidence shading: weeks 1-4 solid, 5-8 slightly transparent, 9-13 more transparent
- Responsive: stacks vertically on mobile with summary cards above
- Use design system colors: #4EC896 for inflows, #E74C3C for outflows, #3B82F6 for net position line

### 3. Create `src/components/financial/CashFlowTable.tsx`
A detailed breakdown table below the chart:
- Rows: Starting Balance, then each inflow category, Total Inflows, then each outflow category, Total Outflows, Net Cash Flow, Ending Balance
- Columns: 13 weeks
- Cells formatted as currency with green/red coloring
- Sticky first column (row labels)
- Horizontal scroll with smooth scrolling
- Each cell clickable to show the source data (which pay apps, which sub invoices)

### 4. Create `src/pages/CashFlow.tsx` (or add as a tab in Financials.tsx)
Full cash flow page with:
- Metric cards: Current Cash Position, Projected Low Point (week + amount), 13-Week Net, Avg Weekly Burn Rate, Days of Cash Runway
- CashFlowChart component
- CashFlowTable component
- Scenario toggle: 'Base Case' vs 'Conservative' (delays all inflows by 1 week, accelerates outflows by 1 week) vs 'Optimistic' (includes pending COs at 100%)
- 'Assumptions' panel showing what data the forecast is built from
- Export to Excel button
- AI insight: 'Cash position drops below $500K in Week 8. Recommend accelerating Pay App #7 submission or negotiating extended terms with ABC Mechanical.'

### 5. Update `src/App.tsx` routing
Add route for the cash flow page, accessible from the financial section navigation.

### 6. Update `src/components/Sidebar.tsx`
Add Cash Flow as a sub-item under the Financial section with the TrendingUp icon from Lucide.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Forecast is generated from real committed cost data, not placeholder numbers
- Chart clearly shows when cash position drops below zero
- Three scenario modes (base, conservative, optimistic) update the chart instantly
- Each week's numbers are traceable to source documents (pay apps, invoices, POs)
- Lowest cash point is prominently displayed — this is the number the CFO presents to the bank
- Works on iPad for executive meetings
- Loads and calculates in under 500ms for 200 line items across 13 weeks"
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Lien waiver tracking is not built — major legal and payment compliance gap",
      "description": "The founder explicitly lists lien waivers as a P0 gap ('Lien waiver tracking is not fully built. This is a major workflow for GCs.'). There is a LienWaiverPDF.tsx export component, but no tracking page, no waiver types (conditional/unconditional × progress/final), no per-sub per-payment-period status matrix, no auto-generation from pay app approvals, and no compliance dashboard. Procore just launched separate lien waiver templates for first-tier and sub-tier vendors. GCPay built their entire business around lien waiver automation. Missing lien waivers can delay payments and create legal exposure. This is a must-have for any GC managing 10+ subs.",
      "prompt": "You are building the Lien Waiver Tracking & Automation system for SiteSyncAI, the most advanced construction project management platform.

## TASK
Build a complete lien waiver tracking module with per-sub per-payment-period status matrix, all four waiver types, auto-generation from pay app approvals, and a compliance dashboard.

## CONSTRUCTION CONTEXT
Four waiver types:
1. Conditional Waiver on Progress Payment — submitted with the pay app, effective only upon receipt of payment
2. Unconditional Waiver on Progress Payment — submitted after payment clears, effective immediately
3. Conditional Waiver on Final Payment — waives all rights, effective only upon receipt of final payment
4. Unconditional Waiver on Final Payment — waives all rights, effective immediately

Workflow: Sub submits conditional with pay app → GC pays sub → Sub submits unconditional confirming receipt → GC includes unconditional waivers from all subs with pay app to owner → Repeat monthly until final payment.

## FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY

### 1. Create `src/types/lienWaiver.ts`
```typescript
type WaiverType = 'conditional_progress' | 'unconditional_progress' | 'conditional_final' | 'unconditional_final';
type WaiverStatus = 'not_requested' | 'requested' | 'received' | 'approved' | 'rejected' | 'waived';

interface LienWaiver {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  subcontractorId: string;
  subcontractorName: string;
  payAppNumber: number; // which billing period
  waiverType: WaiverType;
  status: WaiverStatus;
  amount: number; // the dollar amount being waived
  throughDate: string; // the date through which rights are waived
  requestedAt: string | null;
  receivedAt: string | null;
  approvedAt: string | null;
  approvedBy: string | null;
  documentUrl: string | null; // uploaded signed waiver
  notes: string;
  isOverdue: boolean; // calculated: requested > 7 days ago and not received
}
```

### 2. Create `src/pages/LienWaivers.tsx`
Full lien waiver tracking page:

**Metric cards row:**
- Total Subs Requiring Waivers (count)
- Waivers Collected This Period (X of Y, with progress ring)
- Overdue Waivers (red, count of requested but not received > 7 days)
- Compliance Rate (percentage of all required waivers that are received/approved)
- Outstanding Waiver Amount (total dollar amount of missing unconditional waivers)

**Compliance matrix (the core UI):**
A grid/table where:
- Rows = subcontractors (sorted by trade/name)
- Columns = billing periods (Pay App #1, #2, #3, etc.)
- Each cell contains two indicators: conditional status (top) and unconditional status (bottom)
- Status shown as colored dots: green (received), amber (requested/pending), red (overdue), gray (not yet required)
- Click any cell to open a detail drawer showing: waiver type, amount, dates, uploaded document preview, approve/reject buttons
- Filter by: status (all/missing/overdue), subcontractor, billing period
- Bulk action: 'Request All Missing Waivers' sends notification to all subs with outstanding waivers

**Auto-generation:**
- When a pay app is approved, automatically create conditional waiver records for each sub with amounts from their approved invoices for that period
- When a sub payment is marked as paid, automatically transition from conditional → unconditional (request the unconditional waiver)

**Alerts:**
- Banner at top if any overdue waivers: 'X waivers are overdue. Owner pay app cannot be submitted until all conditional waivers are collected.'
- Warning on the Pay Application page if not all conditional waivers are collected

### 3. Create `src/components/financial/WaiverMatrix.tsx`
The compliance matrix component. Use a virtualized grid for projects with 50+ subs and 12+ billing periods. Each cell is 80x48px with two stacked 8px status dots. Hover shows tooltip with sub name, waiver type, status, and amount. Design system: white background cards, #E5E7EB borders, 12px border-radius.

### 4. Create `src/components/financial/WaiverDetailDrawer.tsx`
Slide-in drawer (from right, 480px wide) showing:
- Subcontractor name and trade
- Billing period and pay app number
- Waiver type tabs: Conditional | Unconditional
- Amount being waived
- Status timeline: Requested → Received → Approved
- File upload zone for signed waiver document
- Document preview (PDF viewer)
- Approve / Reject buttons
- Notes field
- 'Generate Waiver' button that creates a pre-filled PDF from template (use existing LienWaiverPDF.tsx)

### 5. Update Sidebar and routing
Add 'Lien Waivers' as a navigation item under the Financial section with the Shield icon from Lucide.
Add route in App.tsx.

### 6. Create `src/api/endpoints/lienWaivers.ts`
CRUD operations for lien_waivers table. Include getLienWaiverMatrix() that returns the full sub × period matrix in one query.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A PM can see at a glance which subs have submitted which waivers for which billing periods
- Overdue waivers are visually prominent (red) and counted in the metric cards
- Auto-generation: approving a pay app creates the required conditional waiver records
- Bulk request: one click sends waiver requests to all subs with missing waivers
- PDF generation: pre-filled waiver forms from project/sub/pay app data
- Compliance rate is calculated as: (received + approved waivers) / (total required waivers) × 100
- The owner pay app submission is blocked (or warned) when conditional waivers are missing
- State-specific waiver requirements are noted (some states have statutory forms)
- Grid is virtualized for projects with 50+ subs"
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-004",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Budget page lacks real job costing by CSI division with drill-down capability",
      "description": "The Budget page reads from budget_items and change_orders but the MappedDivision interface only has basic fields (id, name, budget, spent, committed, progress, cost_code). It is missing: revised budget (original + approved COs), cost to complete, projected final cost, variance, earned value metrics (CPI, SPI, EAC), and the ability to drill down from a CSI division to individual cost codes to individual invoices/transactions. The getMetrics function in projects.ts calculates budgetTotal and budgetSpent as simple sums but has no variance, no projected cost, and no earned value. A CFO cannot assess project financial health without these calculations. Procore explicitly offers real-time budget forecasting with drill-down capability.",
      "prompt": "You are building the comprehensive Job Cost Dashboard with CSI Division drill-down for SiteSyncAI.

## TASK
Transform the Budget page from a flat list of divisions into a full job costing system with revised budget, committed cost, invoiced, cost to complete, projected final cost, variance, earned value metrics, and three levels of drill-down: Division → Cost Code → Transaction.

## FILES TO MODIFY

### 1. Update `src/api/endpoints/budget.ts`
Expand MappedDivision interface:
```typescript
export interface MappedDivision {
  id: string;
  division: string; // CSI code e.g. '03'
  name: string; // e.g. 'Concrete'
  originalBudget: number;
  approvedChanges: number; // sum of approved COs for this division
  revisedBudget: number; // originalBudget + approvedChanges
  committedCost: number; // sum of subcontracts + POs for this division
  invoicedToDate: number; // sum of approved invoices
  costToComplete: number; // committedCost - invoicedToDate + estimatedRemaining
  projectedFinalCost: number; // invoicedToDate + costToComplete
  variance: number; // revisedBudget - projectedFinalCost (positive = under budget)
  variancePercent: number; // variance / revisedBudget * 100
  percentComplete: number;
  // Earned Value
  plannedValue: number; // BCWS
  earnedValue: number; // BCWP
  actualCost: number; // ACWP
  cpi: number; // earnedValue / actualCost (1.0 = on budget)
  spi: number; // earnedValue / plannedValue (1.0 = on schedule)
  eac: number; // revisedBudget / cpi (estimate at completion)
  // Children
  costCodes: CostCode[];
}

export interface CostCode {
  id: string;
  code: string; // e.g. '03-100'
  description: string; // e.g. 'Cast-in-Place Concrete'
  budget: number;
  committed: number;
  invoiced: number;
  remaining: number;
  projected: number;
  variance: number;
  subcontractor: string;
  transactions: Transaction[];
}

export interface Transaction {
  id: string;
  date: string;
  type: 'invoice' | 'payment' | 'change_order' | 'journal_entry';
  vendor: string;
  description: string;
  amount: number;
  status: 'pending' | 'approved' | 'paid';
  reference: string; // invoice number, CO number, etc.
}
```
Update getCostData() to calculate all derived fields. Join budget_items with change_orders to compute approvedChanges per division. Calculate all earned value metrics.

### 2. Rewrite `src/pages/Budget.tsx`

**Metric cards row (5 cards):**
1. Original Contract Value — formatted currency, with icon
2. Revised Contract Value — Original + Approved COs, show delta from original
3. Projected Final Cost — with trend arrow (up = over budget, red; down = under, green)
4. Total Variance — green if positive (under budget), red if negative. Show as both $ and %
5. Earned Value CPI — show as number with 2 decimals, color-coded: green >1.0, amber 0.9-1.0, red <0.9

**Tab navigation:** Overview | By Division | Earned Value | Committed Costs | Forecasting

**Overview tab:**
- Horizontal stacked bar chart showing Original Budget vs Approved Changes vs Pending Changes
- S-Curve chart (use existing SCurve.tsx) showing planned vs actual spending over time
- Top 5 divisions by variance (both over and under)
- Waterfall chart (use existing WaterfallChart.tsx) showing budget → changes → projected

**By Division tab:**
- Table with columns: Division, Original Budget, Approved Changes, Revised Budget, Committed, Invoiced, Cost to Complete, Projected Final, Variance ($), Variance (%), Status dot
- Each row expandable (click chevron) to show cost codes within that division
- Each cost code row expandable to show individual transactions
- Color coding: green rows for under budget, amber for within 5%, red for over budget
- Sort by any column, filter by status
- Totals row at bottom (sticky)

**Earned Value tab:**
- Three-line chart: Planned Value, Earned Value, Actual Cost over time
- CPI and SPI gauges (semi-circle with needle)
- EAC (Estimate at Completion) prominent display
- Variance at Completion (VAC) = Budget - EAC
- Table of divisions with EV metrics

**Committed Costs tab:**
- List of all subcontracts and POs
- Columns: Vendor, Trade, Contract Value, Invoiced, Remaining, % Complete
- Clickable to vendor detail

### 3. Update `src/components/budget/EarnedValueDashboard.tsx`
Enhance existing component with real calculated EV data from the budget API, not mock data. Add CPI/SPI trend over last 6 months. Add EAC projection line on the S-curve.

### 4. Add drill-down drawer `src/components/budget/CostCodeDrawer.tsx`
When a division is expanded and a cost code is clicked, open a right drawer showing:
- Cost code details and subcontractor
- Mini bar chart: Budget vs Committed vs Invoiced vs Projected
- Transaction list with date, type, vendor, amount, status
- Link to source documents (invoice PDF, CO, PO)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every number on the Budget page is calculated from real data, not a placeholder
- Three levels of drill-down: Division → Cost Code → Transaction
- Earned value metrics (CPI, SPI, EAC) are correctly calculated per the formulas in the industry reference
- Variance is visually prominent with color coding (green/amber/red)
- A CFO can answer 'What is our projected final cost?' and 'Which divisions are over budget?' in under 5 seconds
- Table supports 50+ divisions with virtualized scrolling
